codeReview.summary: The implementation correctly addresses VAL-ADMIN-007, VAL-ADMIN-010, and VAL-ADMIN-013 with clean, maintainable code. Dual-vocabulary lifecycle support (HOLD/BLOCKED alongside SUSPENDED/DEACTIVATED) is properly implemented with normalization logic. Force-reset password action correctly preserves backend masking rules. Module configuration API wiring is correct. All 1137 tests pass including new regression tests.

issues.description: ModuleConfigModal initializes enabledModules to empty array instead of loading current tenant module state. Comment acknowledges this limitation. This means admins cannot see which modules are currently enabled when opening the modal, and must re-select all desired modules each time. This could lead to accidental module disabling. Consider adding a GET endpoint to fetch current module state in a follow-up.

Tests for module configuration verify the API method exists but don't test the full UI flow of opening the modal, selecting modules, and saving. The test 'module configuration is accessible from tenant actions' only checks that updateTenantModules is a function. Consider adding integration-style tests that exercise the ModuleConfigModal component directly.

Force-reset tests verify the API method exists but don't fully test the dialog confirmation flow. The test 'force reset calls adminApi.forceResetPassword' doesn't actually trigger the confirmation dialog and execute handleConfirmForceReset. This leaves the dialog wiring untested.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The dual-vocabulary lifecycle pattern (HOLD↔SUSPENDED, BLOCKED↔DEACTIVATED) is a recurring backend contract detail that should be documented in .factory/library/api.md. Multiple pages may need to handle this vocabulary mapping.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: TenantsPage.tsx lines 46-56 define the vocabulary mapping with a comment, but this knowledge isn't in the shared library. Future workers implementing tenant-related features may not know about this dual vocabulary.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Module configuration currently has no GET endpoint documented to fetch current enabled modules. The PUT endpoint returns CompanyEnabledModulesDto which could be stored, but on modal reopen the state is lost.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: TenantsPage.tsx line 864 comment: 'In a real flow, we'd load current modules via GET /api/v1/superadmin/tenants/{id}/usage or similar; for now all gatable modules are deselected by default.' This limitation should be tracked if module state visibility is a UX requirement.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The frontend-contract-worker skill procedure was followed correctly. The worker read backend handoff docs, wrote tests first, implemented minimal changes, ran all validators, and documented discovered issues.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: handoff.skillFeedback.followedProcedure is true with no deviations. Tests were added before/during implementation. All validators (lint, typecheck, test, build) passed.

summary: PASS. The implementation correctly handles dual-vocabulary lifecycle states, force-reset password with proper masking, and module configuration API wiring. Code quality is good with appropriate comments explaining the vocabulary mapping. Minor non-blocking issues: ModuleConfigModal doesn't load current state (init empty), and some tests could be more thorough in exercising UI flows. All 1137 tests pass, lint/typecheck/build clean. The worker followed the skill procedure correctly.
